sections.name: Viral Testing
sections.description: People with ME may have an onset due to COVID, EBV, Lyme, or Coxsackie infection, but these are often only the first domino to fall before additional viruses reactivate due to a weakened immune system. RNA-viruses in healthy individuals have IgG titres that drop after infection resolves — persistently high titres indicate ongoing infection. DNA-viruses behave differently, with IgG persisting at lower levels even in healthy patients, making significantly elevated titres the more meaningful threshold.

tests.name: EBV Quantitative PCR
tests.description: Quantitative preferred over qualitative — what matters is presence at all. Quantitative testing better at detecting low-level reactivation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Epstein-Barr Virus (EBV), Quantitative PCR
CPT: 87799
LabCorp Code: 138230

tests.name: EBV Antibody Profile
tests.description: Comprehensive EBV antibody panel.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
EBV Antibody Profile
LabCorp Code: 240610

tests.name: EBV Early Antigen IgG
tests.description: EBV Early Antigen IgG — elevated early antigen is associated with active or recent EBV replication.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
EBV Early Antigen IgG, Serum
LabCorp Code: 096248

tests.name: EBV inSIGHT T Cell Immunity Testing
tests.description: Measures stimulated ATP response to EBV antigen-specific stimulation in CD4 & CD8 T cells.

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
EBV inSIGHT T Cell Immunity Testing
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 33217
CPT: 86352 x4
Instructions: Collect 10 mL whole blood in sodium heparin tube (min 7.5 mL). Draw Mon–Fri after 7:00 AM CST, ship same day priority overnight ambient. DO NOT SHIP if holiday within 2 days.

tests.name: EBV by Quantitative NAAT, Plasma
tests.description: Quantitative NAAT for EBV in plasma. Detects presence at any level — better than qualitative for low-level reactivation.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: EBV Quant NAAT, Plasma (3006079)
CPT: 87799
Instructions: Use Lavender (EDTA), Pink (K2EDTA), or PPT. Separate within 24 hours. Transfer 2 mL plasma to ARUP standard transport tube. Freeze immediately and send frozen.

tests.name: HHV-6 IgG, IFA
tests.description: MUST be an IFA for IgG. 1:80 or greater potentially indicative of HHV-6 reactivation; 1:160, 1:320+ most indicative. In a 1996 study, 89% of CFS patients with titers ≥1:320 had active infections confirmed by culture.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: HHV-6 IFA (2013423)
CPT: 86790
Instructions: Use SST or Plain Red. Transfer 0.5 mL serum to ARUP standard transport tube (Min: 0.1 mL). Refrigerate upon draw and send refrigerated.

categories.name: Cytomegalovirus (CMV)
categories.description: No commercial test can fully distinguish abortive reactivated CMV from latent infection. However, a negative CMV IgG can fully rule out abortive CMV infection entirely.

tests.name: CMV IgG
tests.description: CMV IgG antibody. A negative result fully rules out abortive CMV reactivation. A positive alone is not sufficient to confirm active reactivation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Cytomegalovirus IgG, Serum
LabCorp Code: 006494

tests.name: CMV IgM
tests.description: CMV IgM antibody — may indicate recent or active reactivation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Cytomegalovirus IgM, Serum
LabCorp Code: 096727

tests.name: CMV CD8 T-Cell Immune Competence
tests.description: Quantitative assessment of CMV-specific CD8 T cell immune competence. HLA Class 1 subtyping is a prerequisite and a special form must accompany the specimen.

routes.label: Hospital → Mayo
routes.order:
CMV CD8 T-Cell Immune Competence, Quantitative Assessment by Flow Cytometry, Blood
CPT: 86356 x6; 86369; 86352 | Mayo Code: CMVC8 | Not SNY Approved
SPECIAL: HLA Class 1 subtyping is a prerequisite. Special form must accompany test.
Instructions: Green top (sodium heparin), collect 20 mL whole blood. Send in original tube — do not aliquot.

tests.name: CMV inSIGHT T Cell Immunity Testing
tests.description: Measures stimulated ATP response to CMV antigen-specific stimulation in CD4 & CD8 T cells.

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
CMV inSIGHT T Cell Immunity Testing
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 30360
CPT: 86352 x4
Instructions: Collect 6 mL whole blood in sodium heparin tube (min 4.5 mL). Draw Mon–Fri after 7:00 AM CST. Ship priority overnight ambient same day. DO NOT SHIP if holiday within 2 days.

tests.name: HSV 1 & 2 DNA PCR
tests.description: PCR-based detection of HSV-1 and HSV-2 DNA.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
HSV 1 & 2 DNA PCR
LabCorp Code: 138651

tests.name: HSV 1 & 2 IgG
tests.description: Serum IgG antibody testing for HSV-1 and HSV-2. As a DNA virus, IgG persists at low levels long after initial infection — significantly elevated titres are the more clinically meaningful threshold.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
HSV 1 & 2 Specific Antibodies, IgG
LabCorp Code: 164099

tests.name: VZV DNA PCR
tests.description: PCR-based detection of VZV DNA.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
VZV DNA PCR
LabCorp Code: 138313

tests.name: VZV IgG
tests.description: IgG antibody testing for VZV. As a DNA virus, IgG persists at low levels long after initial infection — significantly elevated titres may indicate reactivation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
VZV IgG
LabCorp Code: 096206

tests.name: VZV IgM, Qualitative
tests.description: IgM antibody testing for VZV — may indicate recent or active reactivation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
VZV IgM, Qualitative
LabCorp Code: 096776

categories.name: Enteroviruses / Coxsackieviruses
categories.description: Enteroviruses are one of the primary RNA-virus families contributing to ME. There are over 100 different enteroviruses, so testing for specific serotypes is important.

tests.name: Coxsackie A Antibodies
tests.description: Antibody testing for Coxsackie A serotypes.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Coxsackie A Antibodies, Serum
LabCorp Code: 163295

tests.name: Coxsackie B Antibodies, Neutralization
tests.description: Tests for Coxsackieviruses B1–B6. MUST be a neutralization assay. 1:160 or greater indicative of persistent infection. Inclusive but not exclusive — can test negative yet still positive on biopsy. Persistently high titres ~6 months apart is strong confirmation.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: Coxsackie Neutralization (0060055)
CPT: 86658 x6
Instructions: Use SST or Plain Red. Separate ASAP or within 2 hours. Transfer 1 mL serum to ARUP Standard Transport Tube (Min: 0.3 mL). Refrigerate upon draw and send refrigerated.

tests.name: Echovirus Antibodies, Neutralization
tests.description: Tests for Echoviruses 6, 7, 9, 11, & 30. MUST be a neutralization assay. 1:160 or greater indicative of persistent infection. Inclusive but not exclusive.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: Echovirus Neutralization (0060053)
CPT: 86658 x5
Instructions: Use SST or Plain Red. Separate ASAP or within 2 hours. Transfer 1 mL serum to ARUP Standard Transport Tube. Refrigerate upon draw and send refrigerated.

sections.name: NK Cell Testing
sections.description: In ME/CFS, NK cells are often present in normal numbers but can be functionally impaired — unable to kill viruses and other threats effectively.

tests.name: NK Cell CD56+/CD3-
tests.description: Typical NK cell count test. Can be lower after onset, sometimes very low. Least specific NK cell test.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
NK Cell CD56+/CD3-
CPT: 86357
LabCorp Code: 505016

tests.name: NK Cell Function, %
tests.description: Most holistic / best overall marker to see how your NK cells are doing. May be normal in earlier years of illness, but gradually decreases over time.

routes.label: LabCorp → Eurofins
routes.order:
NK Cell Function Assay STAT
CPT: 88184; 88185 | LabCorp Code: 807502
Send Out to 30035 @ Eurofins Viracor — MUST BE REFRIGERATED
Instructions: Collect 20 mL whole blood in 2×10 mL ACD tubes. Draw Mon–Thu 11:00 AM–5:00 PM CT only. Ship same day priority overnight with cold packs.

tests.name: NK Cell CD57+/CD3-
tests.description: CD57+/CD3- NK cells are highly mature, differentiated, and cytotoxic. Decreased counts indicate increased susceptibility to persistent infections. Potential indicator of chronic Lyme infection.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: NK Cell CD57 (2008912)
CPT: 86356 x2 | Not SNY Approved
Instructions: Use Lavender (EDTA), Pink (K2EDTA), or Green (sodium or lithium heparin). Collect 4 mL whole blood (Min: 0.5 mL). Analyze within 72 hours. Specimen acceptable ambient or refrigerated.

sections.name: T Cell Testing
sections.description: T cell abnormalities can reveal chronic immune activation, where the immune system is stuck in an “always on” state without actually resolving anything.

tests.name: T Cell Activation Profile, CD8 Subsets
tests.description: HLA-DR CD8 T cells are meant to proliferate in response to active infections. The LabCorp range presents zero out-of-range results — a count <25/µL and %<1 is a cause for concern, especially alongside elevated viral titres.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
T Cell Activation Profile, CD8 Subsets
CPT: 86356 x3; 86359; 86360
LabCorp Code: 505750

tests.name: ImmuKnow
tests.description: Measures stimulated ATP response to antigen stimulation in CD4 T cells. A very high result (>450 ng/mL) concurrent with positive persistent virus tests is indicative of an overactivated-yet-ineffective immune response.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
ImmuKnow
CPT: 86352
LabCorp Code: 176120

tests.name: T Cell Fx Complete
tests.description: Stimulated T cell test critical for determining your specific stage of immune exhaustion.

routes.label: LabCorp → Eurofins
routes.order:
T Cell Fx Complete
CPT: 86353 x4 | LabCorp Code: 831367 | Send Out to 403066P @ Eurofins Viracor
Instructions: Collect 8 mL whole blood in sodium heparin vacutainer. DO NOT USE lithium heparin, ACD, or EDTA. Collect 12:00 PM–5:00 PM CT, Mon–Thu ONLY. Ship priority overnight ambient. Must arrive within 24 hours. DO NOT SHIP if holiday follows.

tests.name: T Cell Phenotyping, Advanced
tests.description: Very specific T cell subset test providing a detailed breakdown of T cell subpopulations.

routes.label: Hospital → Mayo
routes.order:
T Cell Phenotyping, Advanced
CPT: 86356 x7 | Mayo Code: TCP | Not SNY Approved
Instructions: Use Lavender top (EDTA), collect 3 mL whole blood. Send whole blood in original tube — do not aliquot. Ship ambient.

tests.name: Regulatory T Cell Panel, FOXP3
tests.description: Direct measurement of CD4+CD25+FoxP3+CD127- regulatory T cells (Tregs). Elevated numbers play a role in continued immune suppression, found in more immune-exhausted states.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: Regulatory T Cell Panel (3002249)
CPT: 86356 x4
Instructions: Use Lavender (K2EDTA) or Pink (K2EDTA). Transport 4 mL whole blood (Min: 1 mL). Analyze within 48 hours. Refrigerate and send refrigerated.

sections.name: Protozoan Testing
sections.description: Babesia, Bartonella, and Borrelia fall into the same diagnostic category as RNA-viruses — meant to be fully cleared after infection, rather than persisting in latency. Typical Lyme tests like Western Blot are not sufficient for a conclusive result. The tests here via IgeneX are the most reliable options accessible in the USA.

tests.name: Babesia FISH
tests.description: Looks for ribosomal RNA (evidence of active replication) directly in blood. Far more accurate than standard PCRs for active Babesia infection.

routes.label: IgeneX (direct)
routes.order:
IgeneX — Babesia Fluorescent In Situ Hybridization Assay (FISH)
Patient orders kit directly through IgeneX (igenex.com). Doctor’s written order required.
Follow collection instructions provided with the IgeneX kit.

tests.name: Bartonella FISH
tests.description: Looks for ribosomal RNA (evidence of active replication) directly in blood. Far more accurate than standard PCRs for active Bartonella infection.

routes.label: IgeneX (direct)
routes.order:
IgeneX — Bartonella Fluorescent In Situ Hybridization Assay (FISH)
Patient orders kit directly through IgeneX (igenex.com). Doctor’s written order required.
Follow collection instructions provided with the IgeneX kit.

tests.name: Borrelia Burgdorferi ELISpot
tests.description: Measures T cell IFN-gamma response to Borrelia antigens. Reactivity dies off quickly once antigen is cleared — a positive result indicates persistent antigen.

routes.label: IgeneX (direct)
routes.order:
IgeneX — Borrelia Burgdorferi ELISpot
Patient orders kit directly through IgeneX (igenex.com). Doctor’s written order required.
Follow collection instructions provided with the IgeneX kit.

tests.name: IgeneX Culture-Enhanced PCR
tests.description: Comprehensive Lyme panel covering Borrelia, Babesia, Bartonella, and Anaplasma species.

routes.label: IgeneX (direct)
routes.order:
IgeneX — Culture-Enhanced PCR (Comprehensive Lyme)
Tests: Borrelia burgdorferi/garinii/mayonii/miyatomoi; Babesia microti/duncani/sp.; Bartonella henselae/elizabethae/tribocorum; Anaplasma phagocytophilum/platys.
Patient orders kit directly through IgeneX (igenex.com). Doctor’s written order required.
Follow collection instructions provided with the IgeneX kit.

sections.name: Cytokine Testing
sections.description: US-based cytokine testing is the most limited area. The gold standard — stimulated cytokine tests where cells are exposed to antigen and the response is measured — is only available in Germany. US tests measure unstimulated cytokine quantities in whole blood. Out-of-range values are definitively meaningful, but a normal result does not rule out dysregulation.

tests.name: Cytokine Panel 13, Serum
tests.description: Unstimulated whole-blood cytokine panel (sIL-2R, IL-12, IFN-γ, IL-4, IL-5, IL-10, IL-13, IL-1β, IL-6, IL-8, TNF-α, IL-2, IL-17). Out-of-range values are definitively meaningful. Inclusive but not exclusive — normal does not rule out dysregulation.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: Cytokine Panel 13 (0051394)
CPT: 83520 x12; 83529
Includes: sIL-2R, IL-12, IFN-gamma, IL-4, IL-5, IL-10, IL-13, IL-1beta, IL-6, IL-8, TNF-alpha, IL-2, IL-17
Instructions: Use SST or Plain Red. Separate serum ASAP or within 2 hours. Transfer 1 mL serum to ARUP Standard Transport Tube. CRITICAL FROZEN.

tests.name: Cytokine Panel, Plasma
tests.description: Plasma-based unstimulated cytokine panel via Mayo Clinic Labs.

routes.label: Hospital → Mayo
routes.order:
Cytokine Panel, Plasma
Mayo Code: CYPAN | CPT: 83520 x10; 83529
Container: Lavender-top (EDTA) | Volume: 0.5 mL Plasma
Instructions: 1. Place tube on wet ice immediately. 2. Centrifuge at 4°C, 1500×g for 10 min. 3. Aliquot plasma into plastic vial. 4. Freeze within 2 hours of collection.

tests.name: Pro-Inflammatory Panel
tests.description: Eurofins-Viracor pro-inflammatory cytokine panel (plasma).

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
Pro-Inflammatory Panel, Plasma
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 403028P
CPT: 83520 x3; 83529
Instructions: Collect 4–5 mL whole blood in EDTA or ACD tube. Centrifuge and transfer 2 mL plasma to transfer tube. Freeze and ship on dry ice Mon–Fri. Must arrive within 96 hours of collection.

tests.name: Th1/Th2 Panel
tests.description: Eurofins-Viracor Th1/Th2 cytokine panel (plasma).

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
Th1 / Th2 Panel, Plasma
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 403026P
CPT: 83520 x7
Instructions: Collect 1 mL EDTA plasma. Ship frozen on dry ice overnight.

tests.name: Soluble TNF Receptor 1
tests.description: Soluble TNF Receptor 1 (plasma) via Eurofins-Viracor.

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
Soluble TNF Receptor 1, Plasma
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 30145
Instructions: Collect whole blood in sodium heparin tube. Separate plasma by centrifugation within 30 min of draw. Transfer 1 mL plasma to sterile tube and freeze immediately.

tests.name: Soluble IL-2 Receptor
tests.description: Soluble IL-2 Receptor (serum) via Eurofins-Viracor.

routes.label: Hospital → Eurofins (direct)
routes.order:
Soluble IL-2 Receptor, Serum
Send Directly to Eurofins-Viracor — Eurofins Code: 30057
Instructions: Collect 1 mL blood, allow to clot prior to centrifugation. Freeze specimen. Ship overnight on dry ice. Not stable ambient or refrigerated.

tests.name: Toll-Like Receptor Function
tests.description: Measures pre- and post-stimulation values for IL-1beta, IL-6, and TNF-alpha upon exposure to PAM3CSK4, Zymosan, Flagellin, CL097, and LPS. Requires a simultaneously collected matched healthy control specimen.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: Toll-Like Receptor Function (0051589)
CPT: 86353 x5; 83520 x2; 83529
SPECIAL: Collect control specimen from healthy individual unrelated to patient at same time under similar conditions.
Instructions: Collect Green (sodium heparin) for BOTH patient AND control. 10 mL whole blood each (Min: 7 mL each). Must arrive within 48 hrs (24 hrs for NY). DO NOT refrigerate or freeze. LIVE CELLS REQUIRED.

sections.name: Signaling Molecules
sections.description: In addition to cytokines, there are other signaling molecules that may be abnormal in ME/CFS.

tests.name: MMP9
tests.description: Non-specific marker of inflammation correlated with vascular remodeling and weak connective tissue. Per Dr. Shoemaker’s range, 85–332 ng/mL is considered normal; above is elevated.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
MMP9
CPT: 83520
LabCorp Code: 500124

tests.name: VEGF
tests.description: Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor — marker of endothelial dysfunction. Per Dr. Shoemaker’s range, 31–86 pg/mL is considered normal; above is elevated.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF)
LabCorp Code: TBD — confirm with LabCorp before ordering

tests.name: Prostaglandin E2 (PGE2)
tests.description: Elevated PGE2 indicates immune exhaustion — has immunosuppressive properties and decreases ability to handle infections. Per Dr. Shoemaker’s range, 200–400 pg/mL is normal; above is elevated.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP → Pan Labs
routes.order:
Prostaglandin E2 — Send out to ARUP FROZEN
FOR ARUP: Send to Pan Labs, PGE2 Code 106
Instructions: Collect 3 mL blood in Purple/Lavender (EDTA) tube. Freeze immediately after collection.

routes.label: InterScience Institute (direct)
routes.order:
Prostaglandin E2, Serum/Plasma
InterScience Institute — Code: PGE2 — Send to InterScience FROZEN
Instructions: Collect 3 mL serum or EDTA plasma. Separate ASAP. Freeze immediately. Minimum specimen: 1 mL.

tests.name: Vasoactive Intestinal Peptide (VIP)
tests.description: Also known as vasoactive intestinal polypeptide. A neuropeptide with roles in immune regulation and circadian rhythm.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide (VIP), Plasma
CPT: 84586
LabCorp Code: 010397
Not SNY Approved

tests.name: Complement C3a, Plasma, Frozen
tests.description: Complement activation fragment associated with innate immune dysregulation. Elevated C3a is a feature of complement overactivation seen in ME/CFS.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Complement C3a, Plasma, Frozen
CPT: 86160
LabCorp Code: 004220

tests.name: Complement C4a, Plasma, Frozen
tests.description: Complement activation fragment. Elevated C4a is a particularly sensitive marker of ongoing immune activation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Complement C4a, Plasma, Frozen
CPT: 86160
LabCorp Code: 04330

tests.name: Soluble C5b-9 (SC5b-9), Plasma
tests.description: Terminal complement complex — a marker of complement pathway activation. Elevated levels indicate ongoing complement-mediated tissue injury.

routes.label: Hospital → ARUP
routes.order:
SEND TO ARUP: SC5b-9 (3017902)
CPT: 86160
Instructions: Use Pink (K2EDTA), Tan (K2EDTA), Royal Blue (K2EDTA), or Lavender (EDTA). Separate plasma within 2 hours (1 hour preferable) by centrifugation. Transfer min 0.5 mL plasma to ARUP standard transport tube. Freeze immediately and send frozen.

tests.name: Neopterin, Serum
tests.description: Produced by macrophages upon IFN-gamma stimulation. Elevated neopterin is a marker of active cell-mediated immune activation.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Neopterin, Serum
CPT: 83520
LabCorp Code: 140335

tests.name: Calprotectin, Fecal
tests.description: Marker of intestinal inflammation. Elevated fecal calprotectin indicates gut mucosal immune activation consistent with gut dysfunction frequently observed in ME/CFS.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Calprotectin, Fecal
CPT: 83993
LabCorp Code: 123255

sections.name: Autoantibody Testing
sections.description: US-based autoantibody testing is inferior to European options. Functional autoantibody testing is best for a proper profile — for comprehensive profiling, Amatica testing is recommended.

tests.name: Anti-Cardiolipin Antibodies
tests.description: Anti-cardiolipin antibodies — presence may indicate an autoimmune component.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Anti-Cardiolipin Antibodies
LabCorp In-house — confirm specific panel code with LabCorp before ordering

sections.name: Methylation Testing
sections.description: Methylation is impaired in some form in practically everyone with ME/CFS. B12 and folate are hard to measure directly — the tests below measure downstream markers as the best available clinical approach.

tests.name: Vitamin B12 & Folate, Serum
tests.description: High serum counts may paradoxically indicate an absorption issue (especially if MMA or homocysteine are also elevated). A low result is a definitive indicator of deficiency regardless of absorption rate.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Vitamin B12 & Folate, Serum
CPT: 82607; 82746
LabCorp Code: 000810

tests.name: Homocysteine, Serum
tests.description: General indicator of methylation cycle dysfunction and potentially both B12 and folate deficiencies. Elevated homocysteine is a sensitive upstream marker of methylation impairment.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Homocysteine, Serum
CPT: 83090
LabCorp Code: 706994

tests.name: Methylmalonic Acid (MMA), Urine
tests.description: Specific indicator of vitamin B12 deficiency. Must be adjusted for creatinine: MMA (µmol/L) / (Creatinine g/L). Can also be elevated with impaired renal function.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Methylmalonic Acid (MMA), Urine
CPT: 83921
LabCorp Code: 716365

tests.name: Methylmalonic Acid (MMA), Serum
tests.description: Specific indicator of vitamin B12 deficiency. If elevated in serum, highly indicative of a true deficiency. Best combined with urine MMA for the full picture.

routes.label: LabCorp
routes.order:
Methylmalonic Acid (MMA), Serum
CPT: 83921
LabCorp Code: 706961
